attack-pattern.description: Short label describing the primary attack technique (e.g. webRequest credential intercept, session cookie theft, postinstall beacon, content-script keylogger).

c2-url.description: Command-and-control URL contacted by the malicious extension.

confidence.description: Analyst or automated confidence score for the verdict (0.0 to 1.0).

crx-sha256.description: SHA-256 hash of the malicious CRX or XPI package file.

description.description: Free-text summary of the threat behaviour and impact.

exfil-url.description: Data exfiltration endpoint if distinct from the C2 URL.

first-seen.description: Timestamp when the malicious version was first observed in the wild.

host-permissions-added.description: Host permission patterns newly introduced in the malicious version (MV3 host_permissions or MV2 match patterns). One entry per pattern. Tracked separately from permissions because broad host access is the primary exfiltration enabler.

id.description: Extension store identifier — Chrome Web Store item ID or Firefox AMO slug. Use the raw ID, not the full store URL, to enable deduplication across events.

malicious-file.description: File within the extension package containing malicious code, as the path appears inside the CRX/XPI archive (e.g. background.js, content_scripts/inject.js).

manifest-version.description: Chrome extension manifest version of the malicious release.

name.description: Human-readable extension name as shown in the store.

permissions-added.description: Permission strings newly introduced in the malicious version. One entry per permission; use the canonical permission string as defined in the browser extension API (e.g. webRequest, tabs, cookies).

publisher.description: Publisher or developer account name as shown in the store.

store-url.description: Direct link to the extension's store listing page.

verdict.description: Analysis verdict for the malicious version.

version-clean.description: Last known-clean version immediately before the malicious update.

version-malicious.description: First version observed with malicious behaviour.

description: Browser extension supply chain threat intelligence. Captures compromised or malicious extension release events, including permission escalation diffs, C2 infrastructure, and analysis verdicts.
name: browser-extension
